ip_intent.one_line_desc: Serial peripheral interface supporting different device modes, suitable for bulk-load of data into and out of the chip
ip_intent.one_paragraph_desc:
SPI Device is a configurable, versatile hardware block that implements three modes (SPI Flash emulation mode, SPI passthrough mode, and TPM over SPI mode) to support a variety of different applications.
TPM over SPI operates in compliance with TPM PC Client Platform, unloading this protocol from a software solution.
SPI Flash emulation mode supports many JEDEC standard commands such as Read Status, Read JEDEC ID, Read SFDP, EN4B/EX4B, and multiple other read commands, allowing OpenTitan to provide, for example, verified boot firmware to other external devices.

param_list.name: SramType
param_list.desc: Sram Entries. Word size is 32bit width.

param_list.name: SramDepth
param_list.desc: Sram Entries. Word size is 32bit width.

param_list.name: SramEgressDepth
param_list.desc: Sram Egress Entries. Word size is 32bit width.

param_list.name: SramIngressDepth
param_list.desc: Sram Ingress Entries. Word size is 32bit width.

param_list.name: SramReadBufferOffset
param_list.desc: Sram eFlash read buffer offset (from egress buffer start). Word size is 32bit width.

param_list.name: SramReadBufferDepth
param_list.desc: Sram eFlash read buffer entries. Word size is 32bit width.

param_list.name: SramMailboxOffset
param_list.desc: Sram mailbox buffer offset (from egress buffer start). Word size is 32bit width.

param_list.name: SramMailboxDepth
param_list.desc: Sram mailbox entries. Word size is 32bit width.

param_list.name: SramSfdpOffset
param_list.desc: Sram SFDP buffer offset (from egress buffer start). Word size is 32bit width.

param_list.name: SramSfdpDepth
param_list.desc: Sram SFDP entries. Word size is 32bit width.

param_list.name: SramTpmRdFifoOffset
param_list.desc: Sram TPM RdFIFO offset (from egress buffer start). Word size is 32bit width.

param_list.name: SramTpmRdFifoDepth
param_list.desc: Sram TPM RdFIFO entries. Word size is 32bit width.

param_list.name: SramPayloadOffset
param_list.desc: Sram payload FIFO offset (from ingress buffer start). Word size is 32bit width.

param_list.name: SramPayloadDepth
param_list.desc: Sram payload FIFO entries. Word size is 32bit width.

param_list.name: SramCmdFifoOffset
param_list.desc: Sram command FIFO offset (from ingress buffer start). Word size is 32bit width.

param_list.name: SramCmdFifoDepth
param_list.desc: Sram command FIFO entries. Word size is 32bit width.

param_list.name: SramAddrFifoOffset
param_list.desc: Sram address FIFO offset (from ingress buffer start). Word size is 32bit width.

param_list.name: SramAddrFifoDepth
param_list.desc: Sram address FIFO entries. Word size is 32bit width.

param_list.name: SramTpmWrFifoOffset
param_list.desc: Sram TPM Write FIFO offset (from ingress buffer start). Word size is 32bit width.

param_list.name: SramTpmWrFifoDepth
param_list.desc: Sram TPM Write FIFO entries. Word size is 32bit width.

param_list.name: NumCmdInfo
param_list.desc: Define the number of Command Info slots.

param_list.name: NumLocality
param_list.desc: The number of locality TPM module supports.

param_list.name: TpmRdFifoPtrW
param_list.desc: TPM RdFIFO Pointer Bit Width. clog2(Depth(16+1))

param_list.name: TpmRdFifoWidth
param_list.desc: TPM Read FIFO Data Width. (TpmRdFifoWidth/8) shall be power of two

interrupts.name: upload_cmdfifo_not_empty
interrupts.desc: Upload Command FIFO is not empty

interrupts.name: upload_payload_not_empty
interrupts.desc:
Upload payload is not empty.

The event occurs after SPI transaction completed

interrupts.name: upload_payload_overflow
interrupts.desc:
Upload payload overflow event.

When a SPI Host system issues a command with payload more than 256B,
this event is reported. When it happens, SW should read the last
written payload index CSR to figure out the starting address of the
last 256B.

interrupts.name: readbuf_watermark
interrupts.desc:
Read Buffer Threshold event.

The host system accesses greater than or equal to the threshold of a
buffer.

interrupts.name: readbuf_flip
interrupts.desc:
Read buffer flipped event.

The host system accesses other side of buffer.

interrupts.name: tpm_rdfifo_cmd_end
interrupts.desc:
TPM RdFIFO command ended.

The TPM Read command targeting the RdFIFO ended.
Check TPM_STATUS.rdfifo_aborted to see if the transaction completed.

interrupts.name: tpm_rdfifo_drop
interrupts.desc:
TPM RdFIFO data dropped.

Data was dropped from the RdFIFO.
Data was written while a read command was not active, and it was not accepted.
This can occur when the host aborts a read command.

alerts.name: fatal_fault
alerts.desc: This fatal alert is triggered when a fatal TL-UL bus integrity fault is detected.

fields.name: FLASH_STATUS_FIFO_CLR
fields.desc:
Set to clear the flash status FIFO.

When set to 1, resets the flash status FIFO used for synchronizing changes from firmware.
The reset should only be used when the upstream SPI host is known to be inactive.
This function is intended to allow restoring initial values when the upstream SPI host is reset.

This CSR automatically resets to 0.

fields.name: FLASH_READ_BUFFER_CLR
fields.desc:
Set to clear the read buffer state.

When set to 1, resets the flash read buffer state that tracks the host read address.
The reset should only be used when the upstream SPI host is known to be inactive.
This function is intended to allow restoring initial values when the upstream SPI host is reset.

This CSR automatically resets to 0.

fields.name: tx_order
fields.desc: TX bit order on SDO. 0 for MSB to LSB, 1 for LSB to MSB

fields.name: rx_order
fields.desc: RX bit order on SDI. Module stores bitstream from MSB to LSB if value is 0.

fields.name: mailbox_en
fields.desc:
Mailbox enable.

If 1, in the flash and passthrough mode, the IP checks the incoming
address and return from the internal Mailbox buffer if the address
falls into the MAILBOX range
(MAILBOX_ADDR:MAILBOX_ADDR+MAILBOX_SIZE)}.

fields.name: status
fields.desc: If set, Read Status is processed internally.

fields.name: jedec
fields.desc: If set, Read JEDEC ID is processed internally.

fields.name: sfdp
fields.desc: If set, Read SFDP is processed internally.

fields.name: mbx
fields.desc: If set, Read Command to Mailbox region is processed internally.

registers.name: ADDR_MODE
registers.desc:
Flash address mode configuration

This register shows the current address mode and pending changes.
It is updated by the HW when the command phase completes.

fields.name: addr_4b_en
fields.desc:
4B Address Mode enable.

This field configures the internal module to receive 32 bits of the SPI commands.
The affected commands are the SPI read commands except QPI, and program commands.
It is expected for SW to configure this field at the configuration stage and release control to HW until the next reset.

Even though Read SFDP command has address fields, the SFDP command is not affected by this field.
The command always parse 24 bits on the SPI line 0 following the SPI command as the address field.

This field has noteworthy read behavior.
If a software-initiated change is still `pending` the sync to the SPI domain, this bit will reflect the value to be sent.
Otherwise, this field will reflect the current value observed in the SPI domain.

fields.name: pending
fields.desc:
SW-originated change is pending.

This bit is 1 whenever the current value of addr_4b_en has yet to sync with the SPI domain.
If an EN4B or EX4B command arrives next, the current value in `addr_4b_en` will be ignored,
and the SPI flash command will take priority, with an update to `addr_4b_en` to match the command's result.

registers.name: LAST_READ_ADDR
registers.desc:
Last Read Address

This register shows the last address accessed by the host system.
It is updated by the HW when CSb is de-asserted.

registers.name: FLASH_STATUS
registers.desc:
SPI Flash Status register.

This register emulates the SPI Flash Status 3, 2, 1 registers.
bit [7:0] is for Status register, bit [15:8] is for Status-2 register,
and bit [23:16] is for Status-3 register. It is SW responsibility to
maintain this register value up to date.

When software writes a value here, it is delivered to a staging async FIFO, where it waits for the SPI side to commit it.
Any updates require at least 8 SPI clocks before they commit on the SPI side, which is the source-of-truth.
After committing on the SPI side, the CSRs will eventually update with the latest value.

fields.name: busy
fields.desc:
The BUSY signal. SW should read back the register to confirm the value is cleared.

Bit 0 (BUSY) is a SW modifiable and HW modifiable field.
HW updates the BUSY field for matching uploaded commands in the CMD_INFO table, when the `upload` and `busy` bits are set in the table entry.

Note that the observable state of the BUSY bit updates every 8 SPI clocks.
This enables continuous polling of the BUSY bit.
However, the passthrough gate (for passthrough mode) only updates when CSB is de-asserted, not on SPI clocks.

fields.name: wel
fields.desc:
The Write Enable Latch signal.
SW should read back the register to confirm the value is cleared.

Bit 1 (WEL) is a SW modifiable and HW modifiable field.
HW updates the WEL field when `WRDI` or `WREN` command is received.

fields.name: status
fields.desc:
Rest of the status register.

Fields other than the bit 0 (BUSY) and bit 1 (WEL) fields are
SW-maintained fields. HW just reads and returns to the host system.

- [ 2]\: BP0
- [ 3]\: BP1
- [ 4]\: BP2
- [ 5]\: TB
- [ 6]\: SEC
- [ 7]\: SRP0
- [ 8]\: SRP1
- [ 9]\: QE
- [11]\: LB1
- [12]\: LB2
- [13]\: LB3
- [14]\: CMP
- [15]\: SUS
- [18]\: WPS
- [21]\: DRV0
- [22]\: DRV1
- [23]\: HOLD /RST

registers.name: JEDEC_CC
registers.desc:
JEDEC Continuation Code configuration register.

Read JEDEC ID must return the continuation code if the manufacturer ID
is not shown in the first page of JEDEC table. This register controls
the Continuation Code.

fields.name: num_cc
fields.desc: The number that Continuation Code repeats

fields.name: threshold
fields.desc:
If 0, disable the watermark. If non-zero, when the host
access above or equal to the threshold, it reports an interrupt.
The value is byte-granularity not SRAM index.

registers.name: MAILBOX_ADDR
registers.desc:
Mailbox Base address register.

The mailbox size is fixed. In this version of IP, the size is 1kB.
Lower 10 bits of the Mailbox address is tied to 0.

fields.name: addr
fields.desc: Mailbox Address. Lower 10 bits are ignored

registers.name: UPLOAD_STATUS2
registers.desc:
Upload module status 2 register.

This register contains payload related status. payload_depth indicates
the payload size (from 0 to 256 bytes).

payload_start_idx indicates the start of the 256B. This stays 0
usually. However, when the SPI host system issues more than 256B of
payload in a command, this field may not be 0. For example, if the
system issues 258B payload, the payload_depth is 256 (as the IP only
holds 256B of payload), the payload_start_idx is 2. SW should read from
2 to 255 then 0 and 1.

fields.name: busy
fields.desc: State of BUSY bit at command time

fields.name: wel
fields.desc: State of WEL bit at command time

fields.name: addr4b_mode
fields.desc: 1 if address mode at command time is 4 Bytes, else 3 Bytes

registers.name: ADDR_SWAP_MASK
registers.desc:
Address Swap Mask register.

This register is used in the SPI passthrough mode. If any of bits in
this register is set, the corresponding address bit in the SPI Read
commands is replaced with the data from !!ADDR_SWAP_DATA.

If 3B address mode is active, upper 8bit [31:24] is ignored.

fields.name: mask
fields.desc:
When a bit is 1, the SPI read address to the downstream SPI
Flash device is swapped to !!ADDR_SWAP_DATA.

registers.name: ADDR_SWAP_DATA
registers.desc: The address value for the address swap feature.

fields.name: data
fields.desc: Desired value to be swapped for the SPI read commands.

registers.name: PAYLOAD_SWAP_MASK
registers.desc:
Write Data Swap in the passthrough mode.

PAYLOAD_SWAP_MASK CSR provides the SW to change certain bits in the
first 4 bytes of the write payload in the passthrough mode.

registers.name: PAYLOAD_SWAP_DATA
registers.desc:
Write Data Swap in the passthrough mode.

PAYLOAD_SWAP_DATA combined with PAYLOAD_SWAP_MASK provides the SW to
change certain bits in the first 4 bytes of the write payload in the
passthrough mode.

The register should be written in Little-Endian order. [7:0] bits are
processed in the first received payload byte. [31:24] bits for the 4th
byte.

registers.name: CMD_INFO_EN4B
registers.desc:
Opcode for EN4B.

If the register is active, it affects in flash / passthrough modes.

registers.name: TPM_CAP
registers.desc:
TPM HWIP Capability register.

This register shows the features the current TPM HWIP supports.

fields.name: locality
fields.desc:
If 1, the TPM submodule supports 5 Locality.
If 0, only one Locality is provided

fields.name: max_wr_size
fields.desc:
The maximum write size in bytes the TPM submodule supports.
The value is the exponent of the 2.

- 3'b 010: Support up to 4B
- 3'b 011: Support up to 8B
- 3'b 100: Support up to 16B
- 3'b 101: Support up to 32B
- 3'b 110: Support up to 64B

All other values are reserved.

It is not recommended for SW to advertise TPM supporting more than `max_wr_size` to the South Bridge.

fields.name: max_rd_size
fields.desc:
The maximum read size in bytes the TPM submodule supports.
The value is the exponent of the 2.

- 3'b 010: Support up to 4B
- 3'b 011: Support up to 8B
- 3'b 100: Support up to 16B
- 3'b 101: Support up to 32B
- 3'b 110: Support up to 64B

All other values are reserved.

It is not recommended for SW to advertise TPM supporting more than `max_rd_size` to the South Bridge.

fields.name: en
fields.desc: If 1, TPM submodule accepts the transactions over SPI

fields.name: tpm_mode
fields.desc:
Configure the TPM mode. 1 for CRB, 0 for FIFO.

If the SW set this field to 1, the HW logic always pushes the
command/addr and write data to buffers. The logic does not compare
the incoming address to the list of managed-by-HW register
addresses.

The invalid locality check still runs based on the invalid_locality
configuration.

fields.name: hw_reg_dis
fields.desc:
If 0, TPM submodule directly returns the return-by-HW registers for the read requests.

If 1, TPM submodule uploads the TPM command regardless of the address, and the SW may return the value through the read FIFO.

fields.name: tpm_reg_chk_dis
fields.desc:
If 1, the logic does not compare the upper 8 bit of the
received address with the TpmAddr constant, D4h.

If this field is 0, the HW uploads the command, address, and write
payload to the buffers in case of address that is not 0xD4_XXXX.

fields.name: invalid_locality
fields.desc:
If 1, TPM submodule returns the invalid data (0xFF) for the
out of the max Locality request.
If it is a write request, HW still uploads the command and address.
SW needs to process the incoming invalid command.

If 0, TPM submodule uploads the TPM command and address. The SW may
write 0xFF to the read FIFO.

Note: The TPM submodule uploads the TPM commands that do not fall
into the FIFO registers (0xD4_XXXX) regardless of
`invalid_locality` bit.

registers.name: TPM_STATUS
registers.desc:
TPM submodule state register.

The TPM_STATUS CSR provides the current TPM status, mostly the buffer and FIFO status.

fields.name: cmdaddr_notempty
fields.desc: If 1, the TPM_CMD_ADDR has a valid data. This status is reported via the interrupt also.

fields.name: wrfifo_pending
fields.desc:
If 1, the Write FIFO is reserved for software processing.

This bit becomes 1 when a complete write command is received.
While it remains 1, subsequent write commands will block at the wait state until it is cleared.
Write 0 to release the Write FIFO back to the TPM module.

fields.name: rdfifo_aborted
fields.desc:
If 1, the last Read FIFO command was aborted.

This bit becomes 1 when a Read FIFO command became active, but the transaction did not complete.
An aborted transaction occurs when the host de-asserts CSB without clocking all the requested data.
This bit remains 1 until reset, or it will clear automatically after the next valid command is read from TPM_CMD_ADDR.

registers.name: TPM_STS
registers.desc:
TPM_STS_x register.

The register is mirrored to all Localities.
The value is returned to the host system only when the activeLocality
in the TPM_ACCESS_x is matched to the current received Locality.

registers.name: TPM_CMD_ADDR
registers.desc:
TPM Command and Address buffer

The SW may get the received TPM command and address by reading this CSR.

registers.name: TPM_READ_FIFO
registers.desc:
TPM Read command return data FIFO.

The write port of the read command FIFO.

fields.name: value
fields.desc: write port of the read FIFO

rtl/spid_csb_sync.sv.doc_comment:
Copyright lowRISC contributors (OpenTitan project).
Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0, see LICENSE for details.
SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0

spid_csb_sync detects the deassertion edge (positive edge) of CSB for
spi_device. With a prim_edge_detector alone, CSB would have pulse width
requirements relative to clk_i, and these could be restrictive relative to
the downstream SPI flash.

Instead, use a CSB-clocked toggle and detect the much slower-changing
toggles, making use of CSB's low duty cycle.
